Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 121. Building a Bath (GPA 241)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ia

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Aššur-bani. Good health to the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka maš-⸢šur⸣—ba-ni

o 33

lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ia

o 44

[ina] ⸢UGU⸣ É—tu-ʾi-in-te

(4) As to the double-door house of the courtyard of the bath, of which the king, my lord, wrote to me: "Is the karintu [...]?"

o 55

[ša ki]-sa-al-li É—ra-ma-ki

o 66

[ša] ⸢LUGAL⸣ be-lí iš-pur-an-ni

o 77

[ma-a] ⸢ka⸣-ri-in-tu-ú ši-i

o 88

[x x x x] ⸢ka-ri⸣-in-tu ra-ṣip

o 99

[ga-am-mur ka]-⸢ri⸣-in-tu-ma

(9) The karintu has been completely built, but [the kar]intu [......

o 1010

[x x x x x x x x]+⸢x⸣

o 1111

[x x x x x x x x]-⸢ru⸣-u-ni

o 1212

[x x x x x x x x x]-⸢kis⸣

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[x x x x x x] ⸢ma-a-ti⸣

rest uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P224587/.
